skill.name: google-news-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically extract structured news data from Google News via BrowserAct API. Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs like searching for news about a specific topic, tracking industry trends, monitoring public relations or sentiment, collecting competitor updates, getting latest reports on specific keywords, monitoring brand exposure in media, researching market hot topics, summarizing daily industry news, tracking media activities of specific individuals, retrieving hot events from the past 24 hours, extracting structured data for market research, monitoring global breaking news.
skill.summary: Extract structured news data from Google News using BrowserAct API for trend tracking and market research.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate news data extraction tool that uses BrowserAct API to scrape Google News. Static analysis flagged external command and cryptographic concerns, but all HIGH findings are false positives. The Python script uses standard UTF-8 encoding configuration (not PowerShell), makes legitimate API calls to BrowserAct service, and properly handles credentials via environment variables. The skill follows secure coding practices for API integration.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains Shell Command Examples
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash command examples (python -u ./scripts/google_news_api.py). These are legitimate documentation examples showing how to use the Python script. No command injection vulnerability exists.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static scanner detected code fence patterns (```bash) in markdown as shell execution. These are documentation examples only, not actual runtime execution.

low_findings.title: Network API Calls to External Service
low_findings.description: Script makes HTTP requests to BrowserAct API (api.browseract.com). This is the intended functionality of the skill - legitimate news data extraction via external API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate API integration pattern. Network access is core functionality for this news extraction skill.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: Script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment. This is the recommended secure approach for API credential management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Proper credential handling pattern. Uses os.getenv() to read API key from environment variable, not hardcoded.

low_findings.title: HTTP Status Code Validation
low_findings.description: Script checks for HTTP 401 and validates authorization. Standard API error handling, not reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard API response validation. Not reconnaissance but legitimate error handling.

content.user_title: Extract News Data from Google News via BrowserAct API
content.value_statement: Research workflows require fresh news data but manual searching is time-consuming. This skill automates structured news extraction from Google News through BrowserAct API, providing headlines, sources, and publication times in a clean format.
actual_capabilities: Extract structured news articles from Google News with headlines, sources, publication times, and article links
Filter news by time range including past hours, 24 hours, past week, or past year
Limit results to specific quantity for focused or comprehensive research
Output structured JSON data ready for analysis or processing
Handle API errors gracefully with automatic retry for transient failures
Monitor task status with real-time progress updates during execution
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key which must be configured by the user
Network-dependent: requires connectivity to BrowserAct API service
Extracts news indexed by Google News only, not full-web coverage
Maximum polling timeout of 5 minutes for task completion

use_cases.title: Track Industry Trends
use_cases.description: Monitor developments in specific industries like AI, clean energy, or pharmaceuticals by extracting recent news articles.

use_cases.title: Monitor Brand Mentions
use_cases.description: Track media coverage of a brand, company, or product over the past 24 hours to assess PR impact.

use_cases.title: Research Competitor Activity
use_cases.description: Collect news about competitors over a week to stay informed about their latest products and announcements.

prompt_templates.title: Basic News Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find news about {topic} from the past week using the Google News skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants simple news extraction on a topic

prompt_templates.title: Time-Restricted News
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the latest news about {company} from the past 24 hours only.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants recent news for daily monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Intelligence
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for news about {competitor} and {competitor2} from the past week. Extract all articles and summarize key themes.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user is monitoring multiple competitors

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract up to {number} news articles about {topic} from the past year. Format as a structured report with sources and dates.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs thorough historical coverage for research

output_examples.input: Find news about Tesla from the past week
output_examples.output: A list of recent Tesla-related articles including: 'Tesla Announces New Battery Technology' from Electrek (2024-01-15), 'Tesla Stock Rises on Q4 Delivery Numbers' from Reuters (2024-01-14), 'Tesla Expands Supercharger Network in Europe' from TechCrunch (2024-01-13). Each entry includes headline, source, date, and article link.

output_examples.input: Get breaking news about AI startups today
output_examples.output: Current AI startup news including: 'Anthropic Raises $2B Series C' from TechCrunch (2024-01-15), 'New AI Coding Tool Launches for Developers' from VentureBeat (2024-01-15). Real-time results from the past few hours with direct article links.

output_examples.input: Monitor Apple news for the past 24 hours
output_examples.output: Recent Apple coverage: 'Apple Vision Pro Launch Date Confirmed' from The Verge, 'Apple Reports Record Q1 Revenue' from Bloomberg, 'Apple Announces New MacBook Features' from MacRumors. Each with publication time and source.

best_practices: Configure the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running searches to avoid authentication errors
Use specific keywords rather than broad terms to get more relevant and actionable results
Set appropriate time ranges: use past 24 hours for monitoring, past week for trends, past year for research
anti_patterns: Avoid using overly broad search terms like 'technology' which return too many unrelated articles
Do not share your API key in public repositories or chat messages
Avoid running the skill repeatedly without need as each execution consumes API resources

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Visit browseract.com and sign up for an account. Navigate to the integrations page to generate your API key.

faq.question: What parameters can I use to filter news?
faq.answer: You can filter by keywords, time range (past hours, 24 hours, past week, past year), and maximum number of results.

faq.question: Is there a limit on how many articles I can retrieve?
faq.answer: Yes, the limit parameter controls maximum results. The skill defaults to 30 but you can request more for research purposes.

faq.question: How fresh is the news data?
faq.answer: Data comes directly from Google News index. There may be a short delay between article publication and appearance in search results.

faq.question: What happens if the API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The skill will detect invalid authorization and prompt you to check your API key rather than retrying automatically.

faq.question: Can I search for news from specific sources?
faq.answer: Use keyword filters to focus on specific sources. The skill supports standard Google News search syntax.
